title: Selected Psalms — Chapter 8: Grace and Kindness

sections.opening: Sit down my granddaughter, I've prepared you a cup of warm tea and lit a white candle. Today we'll dive into Chapter 8 of Psalms — a chapter of grace and kindness that brings success in business and awakens favor in people's eyes. Perhaps you remember from the previous lesson the power of Chapter 7 against pursuers and enemies? Now we're moving to a completely different energy — an energy of wonder and human greatness. Chapter 8 is like a mirror that shows you how special you are, how much humanity was created in God's image. This is a chapter that connects us to our greatness and invites blessing and success to all our endeavors. The candle burns, the heart is open — come, let's discover the secret of inner grace.
sections.theoryA:
Why Does Chapter 8 Bring Favor in People's Eyes?

Chapter 8 in Psalms brings favor in people's eyes because it reveals the inner light that exists in every person. The chapter begins with the words "O Lord our Lord, how excellent is Your name in all the earth" and ends with the same words — a structure of a perfect circle that creates harmony and wholeness.

The wonderful virtue of this chapter is finding favor in people's eyes and success in business. Why? Because the chapter speaks about human greatness — "What is man that You are mindful of him, and the son of man that You visit him". And then comes the amazing answer: "For You have made him a little lower than the angels, and You have crowned him with glory and honor". Man was created almost like God! This is the secret of grace — when you understand how special you are, it radiates from you and people feel it.

In Kabbalah, Chapter 8 is connected to the sefirah of Chesed (Kindness). Chesed is the giving force, the force that flows outward without conditions. When you read the chapter, you connect to this force of Chesed and become a source of light and blessing for others. This isn't artificial or external charm — this is the true inner grace that stems from recognizing your worth.

Tradition tells of Jewish merchants who would recite Chapter 8 before important business meetings. They understood that success in business doesn't depend only on wisdom or money, but on grace — on the power to find connection with the people before them. This chapter opens the heart of those who hear you and creates an atmosphere of trust and goodwill.

practice1.instruction: Stand before the mirror and look into your eyes. Read aloud the words: "What is man that You are mindful of him, and the son of man that You visit him". Now smile at yourself and say: "I was created in God's image". Notice how your gaze changes.

sections.theoryB:
How Does Chapter 8 Connect to My Zodiac Sign?

Chapter 8 connects to your zodiac sign through the link between inner grace and the astrological energy that guides you. Remember from previous lessons the division of Psalm chapters by months? Chapter 8 isn't tied to a specific month, but rather it's a "universal chapter" that works with any astrological energy.

The secret is that the grace Chapter 8 awakens is true grace — not grace that tries to be something else, but grace that brings you to be exactly who you are. If you're a Leo, the chapter will amplify your natural charisma. If you're a Virgo, the chapter will strengthen your precision and integrity in a way that draws people to you.

In numerology, the number 8 (the chapter's number) represents material success and personal power. It's a number of entrepreneurship, leadership, and the ability to influence others. When you read Chapter 8, you connect to this powerful energy and express it through your unique personality.

The kabbalists understood that the grace Chapter 8 awakens is connected to the power of holiness that exists in every soul. In Tarot, this is like the "Star" card — after the storm and struggle, comes hope and inner light. This chapter doesn't just bring you external charm, but connects you to your inner power and allows it to shine outward.

practice2.instruction: Write a list of 3 positive qualities you have. Next to each quality, write how it can help others or bring goodness to the world. Now read all of Chapter 8 and dedicate each verse to one of the qualities you listed.

sections.deepDive:
What's Not Written in Books — The True Secret of Grace

Here's what you won't find in any book: The grace that Chapter 8 awakens doesn't come from what you do, but from what you stop doing. Most people try to "be nice" or "make an impression" — and this is exactly what destroys natural charm.

The real secret is that Chapter 8 teaches us to recognize our inner greatness without showing off. "What is man that You are mindful of him" — this isn't an expression of smallness, but of true wonder at greatness. When you truly understand how special you are, you don't need to prove it to anyone.

Chassidic tradition teaches that true grace comes when you connect to the "good point" in every person. Chapter 8 does exactly that — it connects you to the good point within you, and from that place you can see and connect to the good points in others. This creates a circle of mutual grace.

The hidden virtue of Chapter 8 is that it doesn't just bring favor in human eyes, but favor in heavenly eyes. When you read it with true intention, you merit "divine favor" — the Almighty Himself looks upon you with affection and invites blessings to your path.

questions.question: According to tradition, what is the main virtue of Chapter 8?
questions.explanation: Chapter 8 is specifically intended for finding favor in people's eyes and success in business, through connection to human greatness created in God's image.

questions.question: How does Chapter 8 begin and end?
options: Begins with praise, ends with request
Begins with request, ends with gratitude
questions.explanation: Chapter 8 begins and ends with the words 'O Lord our Lord, how excellent is Your name in all the earth', creating a perfect circular structure of wholeness and harmony.

questions.question: Who is recommended to say Chapter 8 before a business meeting?
options: Anyone who wants grace and success
questions.explanation: Chapter 8 is beneficial for anyone seeking grace and success, regardless of profession or level - it works through connection to a person's inner power.

questions.question: How are the words 'What is man that You are mindful of him' related to grace?
options: They express wonder at human greatness
questions.explanation: The words express true wonder at the greatness within man - when we understand how special humans are, it creates true inner grace that radiates outward.

questions.question: What is the connection between Chapter 8 and the sefirah of Chesed?
options: Chesed is always giving to others
Chesed is a force flowing outward without conditions
questions.explanation: Chesed is the giving force that flows outward without conditions. Chapter 8 connects us to this force of Chesed and makes us a source of light and blessing for others.

closing.summary: We learned about the power of Chapter 8 that brings true grace and success in business through connection to our inner greatness. We discovered that true grace doesn't come from trying to impress, but from recognizing our uniqueness as humans created in God's image.
closing.wisdom: True grace shines when you stop proving and start being.
closing.nextPreview: In the next lesson we'll dive into Chapter 9 - the chapter of gratitude and salvation that brings healing for children and awakens miracles. We'll discover how gratitude creates more goodness in life.

faq.question: When is the best time to read Chapter 8?
faq.answer: Before important meetings, at the beginning of a work day, or when you need to strengthen self-confidence and inner grace.

faq.question: Does Chapter 8 work even for those who don't believe?
faq.answer: The chapter works through connection to inner power and the greatness within man. Even non-religious people can feel the change in confidence and grace.

faq.question: How many times a day is it recommended to read Chapter 8?
faq.answer: There's no limit, but it's good to start with once a day and feel the effect. Quality of intention is more important than quantity.

faq.question: Is there a connection between Chapter 8 and my age?
faq.answer: Chapter 8 is a universal chapter not tied to a specific age. It's suitable for any age and connects to each person's individual energy.
